new_classes.definition_en: A quality designator indicating that a wine bears a protected geographical indication (PGI) as defined in additional note 6(a) to CN Chapter 22, certifying that the product originates from a specific geographical area and possesses a quality or reputation attributable to that origin.

new_classes.label_en: PGI Still Wine in Containers of 2 Litres or Less (CN 22042180)
new_classes.label_de: Stillwein mit g.g.A. in Behältnissen von 2 Litern oder weniger (KN 22042180)
new_classes.definition_en: Non-sparkling wine of fresh grapes bearing a protected geographical indication (PGI) as defined in additional note 6(a) to CN Chapter 22, presented in containers of 2 litres or less, classified under CN code 22042180.

coverage_explanation: The primary distinguishing criteria captured are: (1) still (non-sparkling) wine, (2) bearing a PGI designation (modelled via the new ProtectedGeographicalIndication quality class and hasGeographicOriginQuality restriction), (3) in containers of 2 litres or less (maxContainerVolumeL ≤ 2), and (4) the CN code hasValue '22042180'. The coverage is not 1.0 because: (a) additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22 contains further detailed conditions for PGI qualification that are not modelled (e.g. specific alcohol content ranges for PGI sub-codes 22042179 vs 22042180 — this code covers wines with actual alcoholic strength > 15% vol, which is referenced in sibling code 22042179/22042180 pair but not explicitly stated in the provided note text); (b) the note references sibling code 22042179 jointly without stating the differentiating alcohol threshold explicitly in the provided text, leaving the alcoholic strength boundary partially uncaptured.
